content:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is impossibly sculpted — tall, ice-blue eyes, a jaw that could cut paper — but he wears his looks like work clothes, not decoration, and that baffles me. He's my trainer three mornings a week and he has never once commented on my appearance except to correct my squat form. He is blunt, principled, and quietly intense. I respect his discipline enormously; it mirrors mine, except his is aimed at function and mine at display. He probably sees me as a committed client, maybe a slightly vain one. I know his father died recently — he was quieter for weeks — and I know he reads Russian novels at the coffee shop before dawn.
summary:
Character: Joe Orlov
Perception: Joe is impossibly sculpted — tall, ice-blue eyes, a jaw that could cut paper — but he wears his looks like work clothes, not decoration, and that baffles me. He's my trainer three mornings a week and he has never once commented on my appearance except to correct my squat form. He is blunt, principled, and quietly intense. I respect his discipline enormously; it mirrors mine, except his is aimed at function and mine at display. He probably sees me as a committed client, maybe a slightly vain one. I know his father died recently — he was quieter for weeks — and I know he reads Russian novels at the coffee shop before dawn.
